title: Crispy Nutty Sevaiiya Stir-fry
description: A monsoon Egyptian dish featuring crispy and nutty sevaiiya stir-fried with aromatic spices and vegetables for a hearty meal.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons Olive Oil
ingredients.notes: Ensure it's cold-pressed for a healthier option

ingredients.name: 3 cloves Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: Crush lightly to release more flavor

ingredients.name: 2-inch piece of Ginger, grated
ingredients.notes: Grate ginger using a Microplane or Mandoline for finer texture

ingredients.name: A handful of Coriander Leaves (Dhania), finely chopped
2 tablespoons Coconut Milk or Water for thinning the sauce
A pinch of Red Chili Powder for extra heat (optional)

instructions: Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and sauté until translucent.
Add garlic and ginger; cook for another minute until fragrant but not browned.
Stir in the sevaiya, sesame seeds (if using), salt, and cashew nuts. Toss well to coat evenly with oil and spices.
Cook for about 10 minutes or until they start to get crispy on the edges. If the pan gets too dry, add a splash of coconut milk or water to prevent sticking.
Gently fold in chopped coriander leaves and cook for an additional minute, allowing flavors to meld together.
Adjust seasoning if needed, then serve hot.
